reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via the P2P sync protocol (data_column_sidecars_by_root) by sending a DataColumnSidecar with mismatched array lengths (e.g., column.len=6, kzg_proofs.len=4), causing an out-of-bounds access or crash when the block processor calls verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs → verifyCellKzgProofBatch at block_processor.nim:254-262. The gossip path validates via verify_data_column_sidecar (peerdas_helpers.nim:362-364), but the sync path only checks inclusion proof in checkColumnResponse (request_manager.nim:190), never calling the full validation before adding to quarantine (line 437). Spec reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, invariant PROP-6a4369e9-inv-010: 'Column, kzg_commitments, and kzg_proofs arrays must all have equal length.' Severity downgraded from High to Medium: single malicious packet can crash individual Nimbus nodes, but does not affect >33% of network from one packet (High threshold requires >33% impact, Medium requires >5% which is more appropriate for single-client DoS).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Column, kzg_commitments, and kzg_proofs arrays must all have equal length.
